channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump-backed Ramaswamy wins Ohio governor primary, setting up a competitive Nov. race
item.description: In Ohio, where a Democrat hasn't won an election for governor in 20 years, Republican Vivek Ramaswamy and Democrat Amy Acton could be in for a tight race this fall.
item.content:encoded: In Ohio, where a Democrat hasn't won an election for governor in 20 years, Republican Vivek Ramaswamy and Democrat Amy Acton could be in for a tight race this fall.
item.dc:creator: Karen Kasler

item.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over claims chatbot posed as doctor
item.description: State officials allege a Character.AI bot claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist and provided a fake state medical license number.
item.content:encoded:
State officials allege a Character.AI bot claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist and provided a fake state medical license number.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Scott Turow's latest real-life legal thriller: Suing Meta for copyright infringement
item.description: Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.
item.content:encoded:
Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.

(Image credit: Thos Robinson)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Cruise ship with hantavirus may have seen a rare occurrence: humans infecting humans
item.description: Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.
item.content:encoded:
Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.

(Image credit: Patrick Pleul/picture alliance)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: From ICE detention to center stage: The Texas mariachi brothers opening for Kacey Musgraves
item.description: The brothers and their parents were taken into federal custody earlier this year during a required immigration check-in. The story caught the ear of a country music icon.
item.content:encoded:
The brothers and their parents were taken into federal custody earlier this year during a required immigration check-in. The story caught the ear of a country music icon.

(Image credit: Brenda Bazán for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: The oldest U.S. Jewish seminary, Hebrew Union College, shuts down a rabbinical program
item.description: Hebrew Union College, the oldest Jewish seminary in the United States, was established in 1875 in Cincinnati by the founder of Reform Judaism in North America. The college has other campuses, but its rabbinical program in Cincinnati will shut down and graduate its last four students at the end of the first week in May.
item.content:encoded: Hebrew Union College, the oldest Jewish seminary in the United States, was established in 1875 in Cincinnati by the founder of Reform Judaism in North America. The college has other campuses, but its rabbinical program in Cincinnati will shut down and graduate its last four students at the end of the first week in May.
item.dc:creator: Tana Weingartner

item.title: Midwives saved his mom's life -- and inspired him to pursue the profession
item.description: May 5 is International Day of the Midwife. This year's theme is "one million more" — reflecting a shortage of midwives.
item.content:encoded:
May 5 is International Day of the Midwife. This year's theme is "one million more" — reflecting a shortage of midwives.

(Image credit: Ben de la Cruz/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: NPR went looking for Polymarket's Panama headquarters. It's elusive
item.description: The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.
item.content:encoded:
The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.

(Image credit: Eliana Morales Gil)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: A legal scholar and 'Backtalker' defends critical race theory -- a term she helped coin
item.description: Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw helped name two contested ideas in U.S. politics — intersectionality and critical race theory. Her memoir chronicles the personal and legal framework for her thinking.
item.content:encoded:
Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw helped name two contested ideas in U.S. politics — intersectionality and critical race theory. Her memoir chronicles the personal and legal framework for her thinking.

(Image credit: Carl Timpone)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: The Iran war sent jet fuel prices sky-high. Here's what air travelers should know
item.description: Fuel is airlines' second-largest expense, and getting even costlier due to the U.S. war with Iran. Airlines are looking for ways to cope, starting by hiking checked baggage fees for most fliers.
item.content:encoded:
Fuel is airlines' second-largest expense, and getting even costlier due to the U.S. war with Iran. Airlines are looking for ways to cope, starting by hiking checked baggage fees for most fliers.

(Image credit: Kena Betancur)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
